etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 冘(冘) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") provides the context of being in water, while the phonetic component 冘 (yín, "to go forward slowly") contributes the sound. The character 沉 (chén, "sink") thus conveys the action related to water immersion, specifically the idea of sinking into water.

examples.chinese: 他的船在暴风雨中沉(他的船在暴風雨中沉)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chuán zài bàofēngyǔ zhōng chén.
examples.english: His ship sank during the storm.

examples.chinese: 太阳慢慢沉入地平线(太陽慢慢沉入地平線)。
examples.english: The sun slowly sank below the horizon.

examples.chinese: 他感到自己的心沉入了谷底(他感到自己的心沉入了谷底)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào zìjǐ de xīn chén rù le gǔdǐ.
examples.english: He felt his heart sink to the bottom.
